edges.description: The fusion partner mediates ligand-independent oligomerization and constitutive activation of the chimeric ALK/ROS1/NTRK kinase domain.
edges.source: Clonal Receptor Tyrosine Kinase Gene Rearrangement

edges.description: Constitutive kinase signaling drives clonal myofibroblastic proliferation and an associated inflammatory microenvironment.
Under selective pressure of targeted ALK/ROS1 inhibition, residual tumor cells acquire resistance, driving relapse of the proliferative process.

nodes.description: In patients treated with ALK (or ROS1) tyrosine kinase inhibitors, acquired resistance commonly emerges through secondary mutations in the ALK kinase domain (e.g., L1196M, G1202R, I1171N, G1269A) that impair drug binding, or through compensatory activation of bypass signaling pathways (e.g., EGFR, PDGFRB, MAPK, SHH). This limits the durability of targeted therapy and motivates sequential next-generation inhibitors and repeat molecular profiling at relapse.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and Resistance of ALK Inhibitors in Two Inflammatory Myofibroblastic Tumor Patients with ALK Fusions Assessed by Whole Exome and RNA Sequencing.
evidence.snippet: Acquired resistance to ALK inhibitors is often accompanied by three types of secondary mutations in the ALK kinase domain.

evidence.reference_title: Efficacy and Resistance of ALK Inhibitors in Two Inflammatory Myofibroblastic Tumor Patients with ALK Fusions Assessed by Whole Exome and RNA Sequencing.
evidence.snippet: compensatory activation of bypass pathways is the basic principle of this type of resistance.

nodes.description: A defining initiating event in roughly half of IMTs is a clonal chromosomal rearrangement of the ALK receptor tyrosine kinase gene at 2p23, fusing the ALK kinase domain to an N-terminal partner gene (e.g., TPM3, TPM4, CLTC, DCTN1, RANBP2). Analogous rearrangements involving ROS1, NTRK1/3, RET, and PDGFRB occur in ALK-negative cases. The fusion partner drives constitutive expression and oligomerization of the chimeric kinase.
nodes.id: Clonal Receptor Tyrosine Kinase Gene Rearrangement

evidence.reference_title: Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor: molecular landscape, targeted therapeutics, and remaining challenges.
evidence.snippet:
Approximately 
half of IMTs harbor anaplastic lymphoma kinase (ALK) gene rearrangements, and 
fusions involving ROS1, PDGFRβ, RET and NTRK have also been described.

evidence.reference_title: ALK oncoproteins in atypical inflammatory myofibroblastic tumours: novel RRBP1-ALK fusions in epithelioid inflammatory myofibroblastic sarcoma.
evidence.snippet: each of which had ALK genomic perturbations.

nodes.description: The fusion oncoprotein exhibits ligand-independent, constitutive tyrosine kinase activity. The chimeric kinase autophosphorylates and activates downstream proliferative and survival signaling cascades (RAS-MAPK, PI3K-AKT, JAK-STAT), driving oncogenic transformation of the myofibroblast.

evidence.reference_title: ALK oncoproteins in atypical inflammatory myofibroblastic tumours: novel RRBP1-ALK fusions in epithelioid inflammatory myofibroblastic sarcoma.
evidence.snippet:
ALK oncogenic activation mechanisms were characterized in four conventional 
spindle-cell inflammatory myofibroblastic tumours (IMT) and five atypical IMT, 
each of which had ALK genomic perturbations.

nodes.description: Constitutive kinase signaling drives clonal proliferation of neoplastic myofibroblasts arranged in fascicles, accompanied by recruitment of a dense polymorphous inflammatory infiltrate (plasma cells, lymphocytes, eosinophils). The resulting mass is locally infiltrative with a propensity for recurrence; rare cases metastasize.

evidence.reference_title: Expression of ROS1 predicts ROS1 gene rearrangement in inflammatory myofibroblastic tumors.
evidence.snippet:
Inflammatory myofibroblastic tumor is a distinctive, rarely metastasizing 
mesenchymal neoplasm composed of fascicles of spindle cells with a prominent 
inflammatory infiltrate.
